title: Paleo-Friendly Thai Peas Curry
description: A peppery, flaky, adventurous Thai dish featuring split peas, sashimi-cut, paleo-friendly. Serve with fresh herbs and coconut flakes for garnish.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and saut pan until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and cook for another 2 minutes.
Add the split peas and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add 1 cup of water and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the peas are tender.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon of fish sauce (optional), and 1 teaspoon of ground cumin.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs and coconut flakes.
Optional: serve with steamed vegetables or as a side dish.
Note: if using fish sauce, omit in a paleo diet.
Nutrition facts are approximate and may vary based on specific ingredients used.
This recipe is inspired by traditional Thai recipes, but has been adapted to be paleo-friendly.
Note: if you prefer a creamier curry, add 1/4 cup of coconut cream and stir well.
